headline: Weight down {{PATIENT_SLOT_WEIGHT_LOSS_PCT}} in {{PATIENT_SLOT_INTERVAL}}: Nutrition follow-through is still unspecified.
gap: The plan manages {{PATIENT_SLOT_DIAGNOSIS}} during {{PATIENT_SLOT_TREATMENT}} but does not document a nutrition owner, an intake history, a weight-trajectory review, or a validated screening score. Consider whether nutrition screening or a dietitian referral belongs in the plan; the patient and clinician decide.
question: Is there enough chart evidence to refer to a dietitian now, or should a validated screening score (MST or PG-SGA-SF) and an intake history be collected first?

research.title: ASCO cancer-cachexia guideline (2020): Applicability must be confirmed
research.cite: Roeland et al., J Clin Oncol · DOI 10.1200/JCO.20.00611 · grade A (NUT-03)
research.note: This guidance concerns adults with advanced cancer and appetite and/or body-weight loss. Confirm stage ({{PATIENT_SLOT_STAGE}}) and appetite change ({{PATIENT_SLOT_APPETITE_CHANGE}}) before applying it to this synthetic case. It permits referral; it does not mandate one.

supporting.title: Individualized nutrition during colorectal-cancer chemotherapy: Small RCT (2025)
supporting.cite: Wang et al., Nutrition and Cancer · DOI 10.1080/01635581.2025.2449719 · grade B (NUT-05)
supporting.note: This 88-person study reports chemotherapy-tolerance and quality-of-life outcomes in an adjacent colorectal population, not this tumor type. It does not establish a survival effect or patient-specific benefit.

research.honesty: NUT-09 is an UNVERIFIED gap statement limited to colon-cancer/FOLFOX patients. It cannot establish the evidence gap for this pending vaginal-cancer case, so this template makes no exact-population RCT claim.

quickScripted: Underdetermined: This template does not yet verify a screening score, intake, symptom burden, stage, appetite change, patient preference, or access. Fill and verify those facts before applying the cited guidance or choosing referral timing. This scripted lane makes no survival claim; the patient and clinician decide.

seats.name: Oncology
seats.rationale: The documented weight change makes nutrition risk worth evaluating during treatment, but this record does not establish current intake, symptom burden, stage, appetite change, or a screening score.
seats.requests: A 24-hour dietary recall, a symptom-burden grade, stage and appetite-change confirmation, and a documented nutrition-screening score.

seats.name: Registered dietitian
seats.rationale: Use a validated screening tool before treatment and regularly thereafter. ASPEN 2024 identifies MST (at least 2) and PG-SGA-SF (at least 7 or 8) among valid outpatient-oncology cut points (NUT-02). Weight loss above 5% is a cachexia diagnostic criterion (NUT-04), not a referral mandate.
seats.requests: Weight trajectory, an oral-intake estimate, the screening-tool score, and patient preference.

seats.name: Pharmacy
seats.rationale: Antiemetic and symptom-control adequacy shapes intake, but current supportive-care medications and adherence are not documented here.
seats.requests: Current antiemetic regimen, adherence, and symptom control.

seats.name: Primary care
seats.rationale: Do not assign nutrition ownership from this record alone; confirm the patient's preference and access, and keep the referral a shared decision with explicit follow-up.
